title: GEWUM: General Exploration Workflow for the Utopia of Materials: A Unified Platform for Automated Structure Generation, Selection, and Validation
authors: Jiexi Song, Aixian She, Changpeng Song, Diwei Shi, Fengyuan Xuan, Chongde Cao
abstract: The discovery of materials with tailored properties is increasingly reliant on computational methods. However, the fragmented landscape of existing software often hinders the seamless integration of large-scale structure prediction with rigorous stability validation, particularly in high-performance computing (HPC) environments. To address this gap, we present GEWUM (General Exploration Workflow for the Utopia of Materials), a unified, open-source platform designed to automate and accelerate materials discovery. GEWUM integrates the Selective Random Structure Search (SRSS) strategy with universal Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials (uMLIPs), enabling efficient exploration of vast chemical spaces. Its core architecture features a modular design with native support for SLURM-based HPC clusters. The platform unifies the entire workflow, from random structure generation and diversity-preserving selection to thermodynamic and dynamic stability assessments, as well as advanced property calculations (e.g., elastic constants, thermal conductivity, and quasi-harmonic approximations). We demonstrate GEWUM's capabilities through three distinct case studies: (1) the prediction of low-energy polymorphs in the complex Al-Sc-N nitride system; (2) the identification of a P-62c phase of U3Si5, distinct from the known AlB2 type; and (3) the high-pressure structure prediction of ThH10 at 150 GPa. Furthermore, benchmark tests show reasonable agreement in predicting thermophysical properties. By bridging the gap between uMLIPs and automated high-throughput workflows, GEWUM serves as a valuable framework to facilitate efficient and scalable materials exploration.
summary: This paper introduces GEWUM, an end-to-end automated workflow for materials discovery. It bridges the gap between generating new crystal structures and calculating their advanced properties by integrating machine learning potentials with high-performance computing. The platform automates everything from random structure searching to assessing thermal and mechanical stability. Its effectiveness is demonstrated by predicting new phases in uranium silicide and characterizing complex nitrides.
main_problem: The fragmented landscape of materials discovery software prevents seamless integration of large-scale structure prediction with rigorous stability validation, especially in HPC environments.
main_result: The development of GEWUM, a unified open-source platform that successfully automates structure generation, selection, and property calculation, demonstrated through the discovery of a new P-62c phase of U3Si5 and high-pressure ThH10 prediction.
method: The platform integrates Selective Random Structure Search (SRSS) with universal Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials (uMLIPs) and features a modular architecture for automated thermodynamic and dynamic stability assessments.
model_or_system: The platform is applied to various systems including Al-Sc-N nitrides, U3Si5, ThH10 under high pressure (150 GPa), and SiC for thermal conductivity benchmarking.
key_observables: Convex hull energy (E_hull), phonon dispersion, elastic constants, lattice thermal conductivity, and volumetric thermal expansion coefficient.
important_parameters: Pressure up to 150 GPa, temperatures ranging from 300 to 1700 K, and use of uMLIPs like MatterSim and DPA3-OMat24.
main_assumptions: The accuracy of the entire workflow is heavily dependent on the precision of the underlying machine learning interatomic potentials (uMLIPs).
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows the system architecture, home page, and RD module workflow; Fig 2 shows the screening workflow and energy differences for Al-Sc-N; Fig 5 shows thermal conductivity and expansion benchmarks for SiC and U3Si2.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the GEWUM platform architecture, details the modular workflow (generation, selection, validation), presents case studies on complex nitrides and uranium silicides, and concludes with benchmarking of thermophysical properties.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
